# Summary

The internet originated from 1960s Department of Defense research, with ARPANET's first message sent between UCLA and Stanford in 1969. Key innovations—including TCP/IP protocols (1983), Tim Berners-Lee's World Wide Web (1989), and the Mosaic browser (1993)—transformed the network from an academic tool into a mainstream platform. Following the dot-com crash of 2001, surviving companies like Amazon and Google became industry giants, while subsequent developments including social media, smartphones (2007), and cloud computing made the internet ubiquitous. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and is fundamental to modern commerce, communication, and entertainment.
